title: psorothamnus spinosus
intro: Psorothamnus spinosus, also known as the smoke tree, is a shrub-like plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, characterized by its small, yellow flowers and thorny stems. The smoke tree has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Psorothamnus spinosus include its ability to soothe and calm irritated skin, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of the smoke tree include teas, infusions, and salves, which can be made by drying the plant's leaves and flowers and then infusing them in hot water or mixing them with a carrier oil.
